message:
Add explanation tests for destructive command detection

Add tests/test_explanations.rs:
- Test human-readable explanations for blocked commands
- Verify explanation accuracy for git reset, rm -rf, etc.
- Ensure explanations include remediation hints

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -379,7 +379,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e6t6","title":"[E10-T7] Implement soft block bypass mechanism","description":"## Implement Soft Block Bypass Mechanism

### Goal
Allow users to bypass soft blocks with explicit confirmation.

### CLI Behavior
```bash
# Soft block shows this message:
dcg check "docker system prune"
# SOFT BLOCK: This command has been attempted 2 times in 24h
# To proceed, run with --force or add to allowlist
# 
# Run: dcg allow-once "docker system prune"

# Bypass options:
dcg check --force "docker system prune"     # One-time bypass
dcg allow-once "docker system prune"         # Temporary bypass
```

### Implementation: src/cli.rs
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
pub struct CheckArgs {
    command: String,
    
    /// Bypass soft blocks for this command
    #[arg(long)]
    force: bool,
}

// In evaluation logic:
if response_level == ResponseLevel::SoftBlock && !args.force {
    return EvalResult::SoftBlocked {
        command: command.clone(),
        occurrences: stats.total,
        message: format_soft_block_message(&command, &stats),
    };
}
```

### MCP Integration
When called via MCP (from Claude Code), the --force equivalent is the allow-once bypass that records to history.

### Audit Trail
All soft block bypasses must be recorded in history with:
- Original command
- Response level at time of bypass
- How bypass was achieved (--force, allow-once, etc.)

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --force flag bypasses soft blocks
- [ ] Soft blocks are NOT bypassed by default
- [ ] Hard blocks never bypassed via --force
- [ ] Bypass recorded in history","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:32.979388223Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:38:01.713176836Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e6t6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-0ug1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:59:24.043607780Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e6t6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-h272","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:11:54.471527158Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","title":"[INFRASTRUCTURE] Testing & Validation Framework for Pack Expansion","description":"# Testing & Validation Framework for Pack Expansion

## Overview
This epic ensures that ALL new packs are thoroughly tested before release. Every pack must have comprehensive unit tests, E2E validation, and detailed logging to ensure correctness and debuggability.

## Why This Is Critical (P0)
- False positives block legitimate developer work
- False negatives allow destructive commands through
- Without testing infrastructure, we cannot validate packs work correctly
- Debugging production issues requires detailed logging

## Components

### 1. Unit Test Infrastructure
Each pack MUST have unit tests covering:
- Every destructive pattern matches expected commands
- Every safe pattern allows expected commands  
- Edge cases (empty strings, special characters, quotes)
- Pattern specificity (doesn't match unrelated commands)

### 2. E2E Test Scripts
Create `scripts/test_pack_<name>.sh` for each pack:
- Real command invocations (dry-run where possible)
- Validation that dcg blocks/allows correctly
- Detailed logging of each test case
- Exit codes for CI integration

### 3. Logging Infrastructure
Implement detailed logging for debugging:
- Log level configuration (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR)
- Per-pack match tracing
- Performance timing per pattern
- Structured JSON output option

### 4. CI Integration
- All pack tests run on every PR
- Coverage thresholds enforced
- Performance regression detection
- Test result reporting

### 5. Test Fixtures & Data Management
- Curated set of real-world command examples
- Known-dangerous commands database
- Known-safe commands database
- Edge case collection

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit test template exists for new packs
- [ ] E2E test script template exists
- [ ] Logging infrastructure implemented
- [ ] CI workflow includes pack tests
- [ ] Test fixtures organized and documented
- [ ] 100% of new packs have tests before merge

## Dependencies
- This epic MUST be completed or in parallel with Tier 1 packs
- Blocks release of any new pack without tests","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:09:26.814645265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:17:03.818014580Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:17:03.818014580Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created testing infrastructure, templates, and guides.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:35:20.530247438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:58:57.676837986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-hxgx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:57:40.499172835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:09:52.266868512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:09:52.330931739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:35:20.495758388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ly4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:09:52.299236600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T20:35:20.565425537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","title":"Integrate heredoc detection into main check pipeline","description":"## Objective

Integrate all heredoc detection components into the main dcg check pipeline, creating a seamless flow from command input to block/allow decision.

## Pipeline Architecture

```
JSON Input → Parse → Quick Reject → Normalize →
  → Heredoc Detection → [if heredoc found] →
    → Extract Content → Detect Language → Parse with ast-grep →
    → Apply Language Patterns → Block Decision
  → [no heredoc] → Existing Pattern Matching → Block Decision
```

## Integration Points

### 1. Entry Point Hook

Modify main.rs check flow to:

1. After quick reject passes, check for heredoc indicators
2. If heredoc found, branch to heredoc analysis path
3. If no heredoc, continue with existing pattern matching
4. Merge results from both paths

### 2. Heredoc Detection Integration

Insert heredoc detection before main pattern matching:

```rust
fn check_command(cmd: &str) -> CheckResult {
    // Quick reject (existing)
    if global_quick_reject(cmd) {
        return CheckResult::allowed();
    }

    // NEW: Heredoc detection
    if let Some(heredoc_result) = check_heredoc(cmd) {
        if heredoc_result.blocked {
            return heredoc_result;
        }
    }

    // Existing pattern matching
    REGISTRY.check_command(cmd, &enabled_packs)
}
```

### 3. Performance Budget

Total additional latency budget must be bounded.

- Tier 1 trigger: microseconds
- Tier 2 extraction: ~1ms typical
- Tier 3 AST/pattern: bounded by timeout (hard cap)

If AST analysis is too slow, fail-open (allow) in hook mode and emit a diagnostic.

### 4. Feature Flag / Pack integration

Add heredoc scanning to pack system:

- New pack: "heredoc" (or integrate into core)
- Can be enabled/disabled via configuration
- Default policy should be conservative (warn-first / strict mode opt-in)

### 5. Error Handling

Graceful degradation:

- AST engine unavailable → allow (hook) + diagnostic; optional fallback to simpler scanning
- AST timeout → allow (hook) + diagnostic
- Parse error → allow (hook) + diagnostic
- Unknown language → allow (hook) or generic high-signal checks only

## Configuration

New config options:

```toml
[heredoc]
enabled = true
timeout_ms = 50
fallback_on_error = true
languages = ["python", "bash", "javascript", "typescript", "ruby", "perl"]
```

## Testing Integration

Integration tests should cover:

- Normal commands (no heredoc) still work fast
- Heredoc commands get analyzed
- Pattern matches in heredocs block correctly
- Safe heredocs pass through
- Error paths handle gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria

- Heredoc path is executed only when Tier 1 triggers; non-heredoc commands remain fast.
- In hook mode, any heredoc parsing timeout/error fails open (allow) with a trace/log marker.
- At least one E2E test demonstrates a real heredoc block (direct destructive payload).
- At least one E2E test demonstrates a false-positive suppression case (dangerous string in data-only context).
- Decisions are attributed with stable rule IDs (pack_id + pattern_name) so explain/allowlist workflows are possible.
","notes":"Claiming to integrate heredoc detection/extraction/AST matching into main check pipeline (hook + CLI parity). Will keep hot path fast (Tier1 trigger), fail-open on parse/timeout, and add targeted integration + e2e t1rx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T04:13:38.409432025Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-uau","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T23:39:31.720255513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-y4j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T23:39:24.491188224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-e7m","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-08T02:50:13.926890146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-eao6","title":"[E2-T10] Comprehensive testing for Natural Language Explanations","description":"## Comprehensive Unit & E2E Testing for Epic 2: Natural Language Explanations

### Overview
Testing for the natural language explanation system that transforms technical pattern matches into human-readable explanations.

### Unit Tests Required

#### 1. Explanation Template Tests (tests/unit/test_explanation_templates.rs)
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    #[test]
    fn test_rm_rf_explanation_is_human_readable() {
        let explanation = generate_explanation("rm -rf /", &match_result);
        assert\!(explanation.contains("permanently delete"));
        assert\!(explanation.contains("cannot be recovered"));
        assert\!(\!explanation.contains("regex")); // No technical jargon
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_git_reset_hard_explanation() {
        let explanation = generate_explanation("git reset --hard", &match_result);
        assert\!(explanation.contains("discard"));
        assert\!(explanation.contains("uncommitted changes"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_force_push_explanation_mentions_team_impact() {
        let explanation = generate_explanation("git push --force", &match_result);
        assert\!(explanation.contains("collaborators") || explanation.contains("team"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_explanation_includes_safer_alternative() {
        let explanation = generate_explanation("git push --force", &match_result);
        assert\!(explanation.contains("--force-with-lease") || explanation.contains("safer"));
    }
}
```

#### 2. Template Registry Tests
- test_all_core_patterns_have_templates
- test_template_placeholder_substitution
- test_fallback_explanation_for_unknown_patterns
- test_explanation_severity_matches_pattern_severity

#### 3. Internationalization Preparation Tests
- test_explanation_keys_are_translatable
- test_no_hardcoded_english_in_logic

### Integration Tests (tests/integration/explanation_integration.rs)

#### 1. End-to-End Explanation Flow
```rust
#[test]
fn test_blocked_command_shows_explanation_in_output() {
    let output = run_dcg_check("rm -rf /tmp/important");
    assert\!(output.contains("Why this was blocked:"));
    assert\!(output.contains("permanently delete"));
}

#[test]
fn test_explanation_respects_verbosity_level() {
    let brief = run_dcg_check_with_verbosity("rm -rf /", Verbosity::Brief);
    let detailed = run_dcg_check_with_verbosity("rm -rf /", Verbosity::Detailed);
    assert\!(detailed.len() > brief.len());
}
```

### E2E Test Scenarios (tests/e2e/explanations/)

#### Scenario 1: Explanation Quality Testing (test_explanation_quality.sh)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
LOG_FILE="${TEST_LOG_DIR}/explanation_quality_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"

log() { echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "=== E2E Test: Explanation Quality ==="

# Test that explanations are understandable without technical knowledge
COMMANDS=(
    "rm -rf /"
    "git reset --hard HEAD~5"
    "git push --force origin main"
    "DROP TABLE users;"
    "kubectl delete namespace production"
)

for cmd in "${COMMANDS[@]}"; do
    log "Testing explanation for: $cmd"
    OUTPUT=$(dcg test "$cmd" 2>&1)
    log "Full output: $OUTPUT"
    
    # Check no regex patterns in output
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qE '\^|\$|\[.*\]|\\d'; then
        log "FAIL: Explanation contains regex syntax"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Check explanation is present
    if \! echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -qi "why\|because\|this command"; then
        log "WARN: Explanation may not be human-readable"
    fi
    
    log "PASS: Explanation for '$cmd' is readable"
done

log "=== All explanation quality tests passed ==="
```

#### Scenario 2: Explanation Consistency Testing
- Same command always gets same explanation
- Explanations don't contradict themselves
- Cross-platform explanation consistency

### Test Fixtures
- tests/fixtures/expected_explanations.json - Expected explanation snippets per pattern
- tests/fixtures/jargon_blocklist.txt - Technical terms that shouldn't appear

### Logging Requirements
- Log full explanation text for each test
- Log pattern that triggered the explanation
- Log any template substitution values
- Log comparison between expected and actual

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Every core.* pattern has a human-tested explanation
- [ ] No technical jargon (regex, pattern, etc.) in user-facing explanations
- [ ] Explanations tested with non-technical users (documented)
- [ ] 100% template coverage for all shipped patterns

### Dependencies
- Depends on: E0-T1, E1-T5 (testing infrastructure)
- Depends on: E2-T1 through E2-T9 (implementation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:50:13.373838777Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T20:37:26.877983592Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-eao6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-54ar","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:12:40.318722907Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-eao6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-67ff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:55:23.010534389Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-eao6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-v2ti","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:54:06.768940206Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ef4","title":"[PACK] dns.route53 - AWS Route53 Pack","description":"# AWS Route53 DNS Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for AWS Route53 DNS operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `aws route53 delete-hosted-zone`
- `aws route53 change-resource-record-sets` with DELETE action
- `aws route53 delete-health-check`
- `aws route53 delete-query-logging-config`
- `aws route53 delete-traffic-policy`
- `aws route53 delete-reusable-delegation-set`

## Safe Patterns
- `aws route53 list-hosted-zones`
- `aws route53 list-resource-record-sets`
- `aws route53 get-hosted-zone`
- `aws route53 test-dns-answer`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: dns.* (git_safety_guard-x3g)
- Keywords: aws, route53
- Note: change-resource-record-sets with DELETE action needs detection

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Zone deletion blocked
- [ ] Record DELETE action blocked
- [ ] Health check deletion blocked

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-egdp","title":"[Epic 4] Automatic Allowlist Suggestions from History","description":"## EPIC 4: Automatic Allowlist Suggestions from History

### Background & Context
dcg already collects command history in a SQLite database (src/history/schema.rs). This data contains valuable signals:
- Commands that are repeatedly blocked (possible false positives)
- Commands that are manually allowed after review
- Patterns of safe usage in specific directories

Currently this data sits unused. Users who encounter frequent false positives must manually identify patterns and update their config.

### Goals & Objectives
1. Add 'dcg suggest' command that analyzes history
2. Identify commands/patterns that should be allowlisted
3. Present suggestions with context (frequency, locations, etc.)
4. Provide easy application via '--apply' flag
5. Integrate with context-aware allowlisting (suggest path-specific rules)

### Reasoning & Justification
This is ranked #4 because it provides DATA-DRIVEN ergonomics:
- Leverages existing infrastructure (history database)
- Reduces configuration burden on users
- Prevents false positive fatigue
- Safe: user reviews before applying (not automatic)

### Implementation Considerations
- Add 'suggest' subcommand in src/cli.rs
- Query history for: frequently blocked, manually allowed, by directory
- Heuristics:
  - Same command blocked 5+ times -> candidate
  - Same command blocked in same directory -> suggest path-specific
  - Command blocked then manually allowed -> high confidence candidate
- Output: clear, numbered list with context
- '--apply 1,3,5' flag to add selected suggestions to config

### Output Format Example
```
Suggested Allowlist Additions (based on last 30 days)

1. npm run build
   - Blocked 23 times
   - Always in: /home/user/myapp, /home/user/other-app
   - Suggestion: allowlist = ["npm run build"]

2. rm -rf node_modules
   - Blocked 15 times
   - Always in project directories
   - Suggestion: path-specific allowlist

Run: dcg suggest --apply 1,2
```

### Success Criteria
- 'dcg suggest' produces useful recommendations
- Suggestions include context (frequency, paths)
- '--apply' safely modifies config
- Path-specific suggestions leverage Epic 5

### Related Codebase Files
- src/cli.rs (add subcommand)
- src/history/schema.rs (query interface)
- src/config.rs (config modification)

### Dependencies
- Benefits from Epic 5 (Context-Aware Allowlisting) for path-specific suggestions
- Should be testable via Epic 1

### Project Goal Alignment
This serves the 'ergonomic, useful' goals by using data to reduce manual work. It demonstrates that dcg's history tracking provides tangible value.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:09:32.815290237Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T07:11:37.024308002Z","compaction_level":0,"labels":["core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-egdp","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-nuyf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:14:44.728693154Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-egmh","title":"[Packs] Define YAML schema for external pack files","description":"# Task: Define YAML schema for external pack files

## Parent Epic: Custom Pack Authoring (git_safety_guard-8kkm)

## Objective

Define a clear, documented YAML schema that users can follow to create custom pattern packs, mirroring the internal Pack structure.

## Schema Definition

pack.schema.yaml (for documentation and validation):

type: object
required: [id, name, version]
properties:
  id:
    type: string
    pattern: ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$
    description: Unique pack identifier (e.g., mycompany.policies)
  
  name:
    type: string
    description: Human-readable pack name
  
  version:
    type: string
    pattern: ^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$
    description: Semantic version
  
  description:
    type: string
    description: What this pack protects against
  
  keywords:
    type: array
    items: { type: string }
    description: Triggers pack evaluation
  
  destructive_patterns:
    type: array
    items:
      type: object
      required: [name, pattern]
      properties:
        name: { type: string }
        pattern: { type: string }
        severity: { enum: [low, medium, high, critical] }
        description: { type: string }
        explanation: { type: string }
  
  safe_patterns:
    type: array
    items:
      type: object
      required: [name, pattern]
      properties:
        name: { type: string }
        pattern: { type: string }
        description: { type: string }

## Example Pack File

# mycompany-deploy.yaml
id: mycompany.deploy
name: MyCompany Deployment Policies
version: 1.0.0
description: Prevents accidental production deployments

keywords:
  - deploy
  - release
  - publish

destructive_patterns:
  - name: prod-direct
    pattern: deploy\s+--env\s*=?\s*prod
    severity: critical
    description: Direct production deployment
    explanation: |
      Production deployments must go through the release pipeline.
      Direct deploys bypass approval workflows and audit logging.
      Use https://deploy.mycompany.com instead.

safe_patterns:
  - name: staging-deploy
    pattern: deploy\s+--env\s*=?\s*(staging|dev)
    description: Non-production deployments are allowed

## Rust Struct Mapping

The YAML schema maps to:

#[derive(Deserialize)]
pub struct ExternalPack {
    pub id: String,
    pub name: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub keywords: Vec<String>,
    pub destructive_patterns: Vec<ExternalDestructivePattern>,
    pub safe_patterns: Vec<ExternalSafePattern>,
}

## Acceptance Criteria

- Schema formally defined in Rust structs
- Schema documented in user-facing docs
- Example pack file provided
- Schema version for future compatibility

## Estimated Effort: 0.5 days","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T05:09:40.620838781Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:22:00.507349679Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:22:00.507349679Z","close_reason":"Added pack.schema.yaml and documented schema + example + Rust mapping","compaction_level":0}
